Project.signature: class Project(SQLModel): id, name, path, status, registered_at, last_seen_at, metadata_json
Project.description: SQLModel for registered RAPID projects with their metadata

register_project.signature: POST /api/projects {path: str, name: str} -> {id: str, status: str}
register_project.description: Registers a RAPID project by path, reads its STATE.json and populates database

list_projects.description: Paginated list of all registered projects with current status

get_project.description: Single project with full metadata including latest state snapshot

deregister_project.description: Removes a project from the registry (does not delete project files)

FileWatcherService.description: Watches STATE.json and REGISTRY.json for changes in registered projects, updates database on change. Methods: start(), stop(), add_project(), remove_project()

non_blocking_watchers.description: File watchers must not block the FastAPI event loop; use threading or polling fallback

inotify_fallback.description: If inotify watch limit is exhausted, fall back to polling at 5-second intervals

registration_idempotent.description: Registering an already-registered project updates metadata rather than creating a duplicate

pagination_default.description: All list endpoints default to page=1, per_page=20 with total count in response

definition.scope: Project registration and lifecycle management. REST API endpoints for CRUD on RAPID projects. File watchers for STATE.json and REGISTRY.json changes. SQLite models for projects. The .rapid-web/ sync layer for portability.

tasks.description: Define Project SQLModel with fields: id, name, path, status, registered_at, last_seen_at, metadata
Implement project CRUD endpoints with pagination
tasks.acceptance: All endpoints return correct responses with pagination

tasks.description: Implement FileWatcherService for STATE.json and REGISTRY.json
tasks.acceptance: Database updates within 5 seconds of file change

tasks.description: Integrate SyncEngine to persist project data to .rapid-web/
tasks.acceptance: Project data survives database rebuild from .rapid-web/ files

acceptance: Projects can be registered, listed, retrieved, and deregistered via API
File watcher detects STATE.json changes and updates database
Pagination works correctly on list endpoints
